texto: Part of me felt like I was throwing my life away, for a guy I barely knew. But I wasn’t just doing it for him. Since my parents died, I’d had absolutely no control over my life. If I really thought about it, maybe I’d given up control long before—that day in Oregon when I almost drowned. Since then, I’d always relied on others to take care of me. Maybe it was time to take my life back into my own hands… even if it meant growing fins.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a D.S. Murphy: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Part of me felt like I was throwing my life away, for a guy I barely knew. But I wasn’t just doin…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, folklore, folklore-and-mythology pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de folklore-and-mythology e mermaids.

frase_original: Part of me felt like I was throwing my life away, for a guy I barely knew. But I wasn’t just doing it for him. Since my parents died, I’d had absolutely no control over my life. If I really thought about it, maybe I’d given up control long before—that day in Oregon when I almost drowned. Since then, I’d always relied on others to take care of me. Maybe it was time to take my life back into my own hands… even if it meant growing fins.

texto: Wait—what?” He’d said the last statement so casually, I almost didn’t grasp its significance. “There are merrow who want to destroy the human race?” I pictured a hundred thousand Sebastians, capable of punching through picnic tables and moving faster than we could even see them. It would be a slaughter. A mermaid apocalypse. I almost laughed at the thought, but the impulse died in my throat. I felt sick. I couldn’t believe I’d wanted to kiss him just a few moments ago.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de D.S. Murphy que provoca passagem do tempo e prioridades. Ao questionar “Wait—what?” He’d said the last statement so casually, I almost didn’t grasp its significance. “Th…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre amor, mermaid, paranormal-romance, com foco em paranormal-romance e siren.

frase_original: Wait—what?” He’d said the last statement so casually, I almost didn’t grasp its significance. “There are merrow who want to destroy the human race?” I pictured a hundred thousand Sebastians, capable of punching through picnic tables and moving faster than we could even see them. It would be a slaughter. A mermaid apocalypse. I almost laughed at the thought, but the impulse died in my throat. I felt sick. I couldn’t believe I’d wanted to kiss him just a few moments ago.

texto: I watched the colored lights and sparkles coming off of Sebastian in the dark water, as the ocean began the process of rebuilding his tail. I longed to join him in the water, to wrap my arms around him and let him carry me under the sea; but I wasn't ready. For him, the ocean was a home; for me it was death. Without being able to transform, the ocean depths would suffocate me, the pressure collapse my bones and flesh.
explicacao: Citação de D.S. Murphy sobre temas ligados a amor. “I watched the colored lights and sparkles coming off of Sebastian in the dark water, as the ocean…” articula amor, mermaid, ocean em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como luto e mudanca.
